filters.term: host=172.31.1.25 and apply_overrides=0 and levels=chmlgf and rows=1000 and min_qod=0 and sort-reverse=severity first=1

result.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.7.40, 8.x <= 8.0.31 Security Update (cpujan2023) - Linux

nvt.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.7.40, 8.x <= 8.0.31 Security Update (cpujan2023) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Oracle MySQL Server is prone to a vulnerability in libcurl.|insight=|affected=Oracle MySQL Server version 5.7.40 and prior and 8.0 through 8.0.31.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.7.41, 8.0.32 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: 5.7.41 Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: 5.7.44 Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.name: MySQL / MariaDB Default Credentials (MySQL Protocol)

nvt.name: MySQL / MariaDB Default Credentials (MySQL Protocol)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=It was possible to login into the remote MySQL using default credentials.|insight=|affected=The following products are know to use such weak credentials:
- CVE-2001-0645: Symantec/AXENT NetProwler 3.5.x
- CVE-2002-1809: Windows binary release of MySQL 3.23.2 through 3.23.52
- CVE-2004-1532: AppServ 2.5.x and earlier
- CVE-2004-2357: Proofpoint Protection Server
- CVE-2006-1451: MySQL Manager in Apple Mac OS X 10.3.9 and 10.4.6
- CVE-2007-2554: Associated Press (AP) Newspower 4.0.1 and earlier
- CVE-2007-6081: AdventNet EventLog Analyzer build 4030
- CVE-2009-0919: XAMPP
- CVE-2014-3419: Infoblox NetMRI before 6.8.5
- CVE-2015-4669: Xsuite 2.x
- CVE-2016-6531, CVE-2018-15719: Open Dental before version 18.4
- CVE-2024-22901: Vinchin Backup & Recovery 7.2 and prior
Other products might be affected as well.|impact=|solution=- Change the password as soon as possible
- Contact the vendor for other possible fixes / updates|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
- Change the password as soon as possible
- Contact the vendor for other possible fixes / updates

result.description: It was possible to login as user "root" with password "root".

result.name: Oracle MySQL Denial Of Service Vulnerability (Feb 2017) - Linux

nvt.name: Oracle MySQL Denial Of Service Vulnerability (Feb 2017) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Oracle MySQL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=Multiple errors exist as,
- In sql-common/client.c script 'mysql_prune_stmt_list' function, the for loop adds elements to pruned_list without removing it from the existing list.
- If application gets disconnected just before it tries to prepare a new statement, 'mysql_prune_stmt_list' tries to detach all previously prepared statements.|affected=Oracle MySQL version before 5.6.21 and 5.7.x before 5.7.5 on Linux|impact=Successful exploitation of this vulnerability will allow attackers to cause crash of applications using that MySQL client.|solution=Upgrade to Oracle MySQL version 5.6.21 or 5.7.5 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Oracle MySQL version 5.6.21 or 5.7.5 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: 5.6.21 Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.6.42 / 5.7 <= 5.7.24 / 8.0 <= 8.0.13 Security Update (cpujan2019) - Linux

nvt.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.6.42 / 5.7 <= 5.7.24 / 8.0 <= 8.0.13 Security Update (cpujan2019) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N|summary=Oracle MySQL Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The attacks range in variety and difficulty. Most of them allow an attacker with network access via multiple protocols to compromise the MySQL Server.
For further information refer to the official advisory via the referenced link.|affected=Oracle MySQL Server versions 5.6.42 and prior, 5.7 through 5.7.24 and 8.0 through 8.0.13.|impact=Successful exploitation of this vulnerability can result in unauthorized access to critical data or complete access to all MySQL Server accessible data and unauthorized ability to cause a hang or frequently repeatable crash (complete DOS) of MySQL Server.|solution=Updates are available. Apply the necessary patch from the referenced link.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Updates are available. Apply the necessary patch from the referenced link.

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: Apply the patch Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.name: Oracle MySQL Backronym Vulnerability (Jun 2016) - Linux

nvt.name: Oracle MySQL Backronym Vulnerability (Jun 2016) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=Oracle MySQL is prone to a backronym vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to improper validation of MySQL client library when establishing a secure connection to a MySQL server using the --ssl option.|affected=Oracle MySQL Server 5.7.2 and earlier on Linux.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof servers via a cleartext-downgrade attack.|solution=Upgrade to version Oracle MySQL Server 5.7.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to version Oracle MySQL Server 5.7.3 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: 5.7.3 Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.7.42, 8.x <= 8.0.33 Security Update (cpujul2023) - Linux

nvt.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.6.42 / 5.7 <= 5.7.24 / 8.0 <= 8.0.13 Security Update (cpuapr2019) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Oracle MySQL Server is prone to a vulnerability in the libmysqld subcomponent.|insight=Difficult to exploit vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access via multiple protocols to compromise MySQL Server. Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in unauthorized access to critical data or complete access to all MySQL Server accessible data.|affected=Oracle MySQL Server versions 5.6.42 and prior, 5.7 through 5.7.24 and 8.0 through 8.0.13.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.6.43, 5.7.25, 8.0.14 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: 5.6.43 Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 8.0.43, 8.1.x <= 8.4.7, 9.0.0 <= 9.4.0 Security Update (cpuoct2025) - Linux

nvt.name: Oracle MySQL Server <= 8.0.43, 8.1.x <= 8.4.7, 9.0.0 <= 9.4.0 Security Update (cpuoct2025) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:H|summary=Oracle MySQL Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=|affected=Oracle MySQL Server versions 8.0.43 and prior, 8.1.x through 8.4.6 and 9.0.0 through 9.4.0.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that all versions prior to 8.x and versions like 9.2.x in between are also affected by these flaws. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.0.44, 8.4.7, 9.4.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 5.5.62 Fixed version: 8.0.44 Installation path / port: 3306/tcp

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 2259507992 Packet 2: 2259509120

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

result.name: Database Open Access Information Disclosure Vulnerability

nvt.name: Database Open Access Information Disclosure Vulnerability
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Various Database server might be prone to an information disclosure vulnerability if accessible to remote systems.|insight=The remote database server is not restricting direct access from remote systems.|affected=- Oracle MySQL
- MariaDB
- IBM DB2
- PostgreSQL
- IBM solidDB
- Oracle Database
- Microsoft SQL Server|impact=Successful exploitation could allow an attacker to obtain sensitive information from the database.|solution=Restrict database access to remote systems. Please see the manual of the affected database server for more information.|vuldetect=Checks the result of various database server detections and evaluates their results.|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution: Restrict database access to remote systems. Please see the manual of the affected database server for more information.

result.description: Oracle MySQL can be accessed by remote attackers

result.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

nvt.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.|solution_type=

result.description: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.25):
172.30.0.2 172.31.1.25
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: Services

nvt.name: Services
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This plugin performs service detection.|insight=This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: A MySQL server is running on this port

result.name: CPE Inventory

nvt.name: CPE Inventory
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: 172.31.1.25|cpe:/a:mysql:mysql:5.5.62 172.31.1.25|cpe:/a:oracle:mysql:5.5.62 172.31.1.25|cpe:/o:linux:kernel

result.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

nvt.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Best matching OS:
OS: Linux Kernel CPE: cpe:/o:linux:kernel Found by VT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.102002 (Operating System (OS) Detection (ICMP)) Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

result.name: Hostname Determination Reporting

nvt.name: Hostname Determination Reporting
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Hostname determination for IP 172.31.1.25:
Hostname|Source mysql55.trabalho_vulnnet|Reverse-DNS

result.name: MariaDB / Oracle MySQL Detection (MySQL Protocol)

nvt.name: MariaDB / Oracle MySQL Detection (MySQL Protocol)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=MySQL protocol-based detection of MariaDB / Oracle MySQL.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Detected Oracle MySQL
Version: 5.5.62 Location: 3306/tcp CPE: cpe:/a:oracle:mysql:5.5.62
Concluded from version/product identification result: 5.5.62

nvt.name: Generic HTTP Directory Traversal / File Inclusion (Web Root) - Active Check
HTTP Server Banner Enumeration
HTTP Server Banner Enumeration
HTTP Server Banner Enumeration
FCKeditor Detection (HTTP)
HTTP TRACE Method Enabled
